most_useful: Traffic-light risk tier badge made it instantly clear who to prioritise
confusing: The IMD decile slider was unclear at first - had to read the helper text to know 1 meant most deprived
shap_helpful: Yes - the plain-English factor labels were easy to explain to a colleague
interventions_relevant: Yes - transport and carer cards matched what I would actually do
notes: Fast once I understood the deprivation slider; Bias Monitoring was not assigned because staff cannot access admin governance views

most_useful: Dashboard stats and the recent assessments list - good for weekly planning
confusing: Nothing major; wanted a bulk upload but understood it is out of scope for the prototype
interventions_relevant: Yes - clinical triage escalation is exactly what I would flag
notes: Very efficient workflow; admin access allowed the governance audit task

most_useful: Bias monitoring dashboard with demographic parity and equalised odds metrics
confusing: The equalised odds metric needed the tooltip - not obvious to a non-technical user at first glance
shap_helpful: Yes - SHAP made the model defensible for governance
notes: Reassuring that fairness is built in; admin access matched the governance responsibility

most_useful: SHAP explanation card showing why a patient is high risk
confusing: Bias Monitoring was not available to the staff role; this matched the governance access model
interventions_relevant: Yes - welfare call and enhanced reminder cards are practical
notes: Role gating worked correctly for staff access; otherwise smooth on tablet

most_useful: Intervention recommendation cards - told me exactly what action to take
confusing: On mobile the assessment form was long; some clinical flag checkboxes needed scrolling and a couple of fields felt cramped at 375px
notes: Works on mobile but the form could be split into steps; Bias Monitoring was not assigned for staff access
